title: Core game
summary: How a turn flows, the actions a player may take, and the basic movement tests.

sections.heading: Turn structure & turnovers
sections.body: Players activate one at a time; each declares one Action, resolves it, then the next may go — until everyone who wants to has acted, or a [[turnover|Turnover]] ends the turn. A Turnover (forfeiting any not-yet-activated players' go) is caused by, among other things: a failed pick-up, a fumbled or intercepted Pass, the ball ending with the opposition, or an active player pushed into the crowd or sent off.

sections.heading: Team re-rolls
sections.body: Once per team turn a coach may spend a Team Re-roll to re-roll a single failed roll: every die of that roll is thrown again and the second result stands — a re-roll can't itself be re-rolled. A team's re-rolls are a limited pool that refreshes at half-time, and a handful of rolls (scatter, armour, injury, casualty, throw-ins) can't be team-re-rolled. A player with the [[loner|Loner]] trait isn't a reliable team player and may waste the re-roll.

sections.heading: Player actions
sections.body: A player takes exactly one Action per activation — one of the nine standard actions (Move, Secure the Ball, Block, Blitz, Pass, Hand-off, Throw Team-mate, Foul, or Forego Activation), or a Special action granted by a skill or trait. Move and Block are unlimited per turn; the Blitz, Pass, Hand-off, Foul, Throw Team-mate and Secure the Ball actions are each capped at once per team turn. Forego Activation simply skips the player without acting.

sections.heading: Movement
sections.body: A player may move up to their MA in squares, one at a time, in any direction (diagonals included). Plain movement needs no roll; a player can't enter an occupied square or voluntarily leave the pitch.

sections.heading: Rushing
sections.body: To move beyond MA, each extra square is a [[rush|Rush]]: roll 2+ (a natural 1 falls over, ending the activation). At most two Rushes per activation. If a square needs both a Rush and a Dodge, the Rush is rolled first.

sections.heading: Dodging
sections.body: Leaving a square where the player is [[marked|Marked]] needs a Dodge — an Agility test against AG, with −1 for each opponent Marking the square being entered. Success: the player moves and stays standing, and may keep moving. Failure: they fall over in the target square — a [[turnover|Turnover]] if they were carrying the ball.

sections.heading: Jumping
sections.body: A player may Jump over a single [[prone|Prone]] or [[stunned|Stunned]] player in their path for 2 squares of MA: an Agility test, −1 per opponent Marking the starting or landing square (whichever has more). Success lands the player Standing and they may keep moving; failure falls them in the landing square — a natural 1 falls in the starting square — and ends the activation.

sections.heading: Picking up the ball
sections.body: Entering the ball's square during a Move is an Agility test, −1 per opponent [[marked|Marking]] the picker-up; failure is a [[turnover|Turnover]] and the ball bounces. The Secure the Ball action (new in Third Season) lets a player clear of nearby opponents make a free move to the ball and roll 2+ to collect it automatically (a natural 1 is a Turnover) — a reliable pick-up for low-Agility teams.

sections.heading: Standing up
sections.body: Standing from [[prone|Prone]] costs 3 squares of MA before the player does anything else. A player with MA 2 or less rolls instead: 4+ to stand (spending the whole move); on 1–3 they stay prone and the activation ends.

title: The drive
summary: Tackle zones, player states, scoring, the turn structure, and recovering knocked-out players.

sections.heading: Tackle zones & marking
sections.body: A standing player projects a [[tackle-zone|tackle zone]] over the eight squares around it. A standing player in an opponent's tackle zone is [[marked|Marked]] — what forces a Dodge to leave the square and adds the −1-per-marker penalties on pick-ups, passes and catches. [[prone|Prone]] and [[stunned|Stunned]] players have no tackle zone.

sections.heading: Player states
sections.body:
Standing — acts normally.
Prone — knocked down, face-up; can't act; stands for 3 MA (4+ at MA ≤ 2); no tackle zone.
Stunned — face-down; can't be activated; turned face-up to [[prone|Prone]] at the end of its team's turn.
Distracted — can't catch or give [[assist|assists]]; becomes Standing at the end of its team's turn.

sections.heading: Scoring a touchdown
sections.body: A standing player carrying the ball who ends a move in the opposing end zone scores. The drive ends immediately, both teams set up again, and the team that just scored kicks off to restart.

sections.heading: Stalling
sections.body: A team with the ball and a clear path to score may hold off — stalling to run down the clock. If the referee judges a ball carrier is deliberately stalling, the crowd takes a hand, and random interference can disrupt the stalling team.

sections.heading: Drives, turns & halves
sections.body: A game is 2 halves. Each half is 8 rounds; a round is 2 turns (one per coach) — 8 turns per team per half, 16 per game. A drive runs from a kick-off until a touchdown is scored or the half ends.

sections.heading: Knock-out recovery
sections.body: At the end of each drive, each coach rolls a D6 for every KO'd player in their Reserves; on a 4+ the player recovers and is available next drive (some inducements and weather modify the roll).

sections.heading: Ending the game
sections.body: After 16 rounds the team with the most touchdowns wins. A drawn knockout match goes to extra time — one more round each, played sudden-death, the first team to take the lead winning; a tie even then is settled by penalty kicks.

title: Blocking
summary: Throwing a Block, how Strength and assists set the dice, and resolving a knock-down through armour and injury.

sections.heading: The Block action
sections.body: A Block pits the blocker's ST against an adjacent opponent's. Roll the Block dice, apply one result, then resolve any [[knocked-down|knock-down]]. A [[blitz|Blitz]] is a Block combined with movement, limited to once per team turn.

sections.heading: Strength & number of block dice
sections.body: Compare the blocker's ST to the target's, after [[assist|assists]]: equal rolls 1 die, one player stronger rolls 2, one player at least double rolls 3. With 2 or 3 dice the coach of the stronger player chooses which result applies — so blocking up into a stronger player still rolls 2–3 dice, but the defender picks.

sections.heading: Assists
sections.body:
Each [[assist|assist]] grants +1 ST.
Offensive assist — another of your standing players is also [[marked|Marking]] the target and is not itself Marked → +1 ST to the blocker.
Defensive assist — one of the target's standing team-mates is Marking the blocker and is not itself Marked → +1 ST to the target.

sections.heading: Block dice results
sections.body:
Player Down — the blocker is [[knocked-down|Knocked Down]].
[[both-down|Both Down]] — both players are Knocked Down.
[[push-back|Push Back]] — the target is pushed back one square; the blocker may [[follow-up|Follow-up]].
Stumble — if the target has [[dodge|Dodge]], treat as Push Back; otherwise as POW!
[[pow|POW!]] — the target is pushed back one square, then Knocked Down.

sections.heading: Push backs & follow-up
sections.body: A [[push-back|push]] moves the target one square away; the blocker may [[follow-up|Follow-up]] into the vacated square (free, no MA), decided before any armour roll. If there is no empty square, the pushed player chain-pushes whoever occupies the destination, and so on; the blocking coach chooses the directions. A player pushed off the pitch edge is removed and risks injury (a Throw-in if they carried the ball; a [[turnover|Turnover]] if they were on the active team).

sections.heading: Knock-down → armour → injury
sections.body: A [[knocked-down|Knocked Down]] player checks armour, then possibly injury. Armour — roll 2D6; if the total is ≥ the player's AV, armour is broken and an injury roll follows. Injury — roll 2D6: 2–7 [[stunned|Stunned]] (recovers on the pitch), 8–9 Knocked-out (to the Reserves), 10–12 a [[casualty|Casualty]] resolved on the Injuries page; Stunty players use a harsher table.

sections.heading: Pushed into the crowd
sections.body: A player pushed off the pitch edge is dragged into the crowd: roll immediately on the injury table, with no armour roll. A [[stunned|Stunned]] result sends them to the Reserves box rather than lying on the pitch. The ball, if they held it, is thrown back in; and if the player was on the active team, it is a [[turnover|Turnover]].

sections.heading: Apothecary
sections.body: A team with an apothecary may use it once per game on one of its own knocked-out or [[casualty|Casualty]] players. On a knocked-out player it patches them up to merely [[stunned|Stunned]] in place; on a Casualty the coach re-rolls the Casualty result and keeps the better. Some players — Secret Weapons among them — can't be treated.

title: Passing
summary: Throwing the ball, catching it, handing off, and what a loose ball does.

sections.heading: The Pass action
sections.body:
Once per turn, throw the ball at a target square: the thrower makes a Passing test against their PA, modified by range — Quick Pass 0, Short Pass −1, Long Pass −2, Long Bomb −3 — and −1 for each opponent [[marked|Marking]] the thrower.
Accurate (test passed, or a natural 6): the ball lands in the target square for a team-mate to Catch. Inaccurate (failed): it scatters before landing. Fumble (modified 1, or natural 1): the ball is dropped, bounces from the thrower's square, and a [[turnover|Turnover]] is caused.

sections.heading: Catching
sections.body: The receiver makes an Agility test, with −1 if the ball had bounced, −1 if it was thrown in, and −1 per opponent [[marked|Marking]] the catcher. Success (or a natural 6) gives possession; failure (or a natural 1) makes the ball bounce. A [[prone|Prone]], [[stunned|Stunned]] or [[distracted|Distracted]] player auto-fails a catch.

sections.heading: Hand-off
sections.body: A once-per-turn action: the ball carrier finishes their move adjacent to a standing team-mate who still has their [[tackle-zone|tackle zone]], and that team-mate makes a Catch.

sections.heading: Loose balls
sections.body: Bounce: a loose ball moves one square in a random direction. Scatter: an inaccurate pass deviates several squares from its target before landing. Throw-in: a ball leaving the pitch is placed on the last square it occupied, then rolls a D6 for direction (a D3 at a corner) and travels 2D6 squares.

sections.heading: Throw-ins
sections.body: When the ball leaves the pitch the crowd throws it back in from the last square it was over: roll for a direction (a wider spread from a corner) and the ball travels 2D6 squares before bouncing. A player whose square it reaches may catch it, with the throw-in penalty.

sections.heading: Throw Team-mate
sections.body: Requires the [[throw-team-mate|Throw Team-mate]] trait. Once per turn, the thrower picks up an adjacent team-mate who has [[right-stuff|Right Stuff]] and throws them; the target must lie within the first two bands of the Range Ruler.

title: Fouling & interceptions
summary: Kicking a downed opponent, and snatching a thrown pass out of the air.

sections.heading: The Foul action
sections.body:
A once-per-turn dirty hit on a [[prone|Prone]] or [[stunned|Stunned]] opponent: the fouler may move first, then fouls and can't move afterwards. It isn't a Block — just a straight Armour roll against the downed player (and an Injury roll if it breaks), modified by [[assist|assists]]: +1 per offensive assist (your players marking the target), −1 per defensive assist (their players marking your fouler).
A natural double on the Armour or Injury roll spotted by the ref gets the fouler sent off once the foul resolves, causing a [[turnover|Turnover]] if it was the active team. Argue the Call (D6): 1 ejects the coach for the rest of the game; 2–5 the send-off stands; 6 reinstates the player (the Turnover still stands).

sections.heading: Interception
sections.body:
When a pass is thrown and the Range Ruler crosses a square holding a standing opposition player, that coach may pick one to attempt an interception — an Agility test at −3 against an accurate pass or −2 against an inaccurate one, plus −1 per opponent [[marked|Marking]] the interceptor.
Success (or a natural 6) takes the ball and causes a [[turnover|Turnover]]; failure lets the pass carry on as normal.

title: Setup & kick-off
summary: Setting up both teams, the coin toss, and kicking off to begin a drive.

sections.heading: Team setup
sections.body: Both teams set up in their own half, up to 11 players each: the kicking team first, then the receiving team. At least 3 players must stand on the Line of Scrimmage (the central squares adjacent to the line), and no more than 2 may stand in each Wide Zone.

sections.heading: Coin toss
sections.body: A roll-off decides who starts: the winner chooses whether to kick or receive.

sections.heading: The kick
sections.body: The kicking team places the ball in any square of the opponent's half, then it deviates to the square it lands in. Touchback — if the ball leaves the pitch or crosses back over the Line of Scrimmage, the receiving coach gives it to a standing player (or empty square) in their own half. The kick then triggers a kick-off event (see the Kick-off page).

title: Pre-game
summary: What happens between fixtures and kick-off — filling the squad, levelling the odds, and a plea to the dice god.

sections.heading: Pre-game sequence
sections.body: Before the coin toss and kick-off, a league fixture runs three steps. First, each team takes on Journeymen to fill its squad out to 11. Then the coach with the lower Team Value spends the gap on star players and inducements. Finally, the underdog may pray to Nuffle for a blessing (see the Prayers to Nuffle page).

sections.heading: Journeymen
sections.body: A team with fewer than 11 available players takes on Journeymen — free, 0-cost Linemen from its own roster with the [[loner|Loner]] (4+) trait — enough to reach 11. They can push the squad over 16 temporarily (counting injured players), but never more than 11 able to take the field.

sections.heading: Special play cards
sections.body: Special play cards are not part of the core Third Season Edition game; some house and league formats add their own card decks.

title: Post-game & leagues
summary: What happens after a match: winnings, fan changes, and how players earn and spend Star Player Points.

sections.heading: Post-game sequence
sections.body:
Resolved in order:
1. Record the outcome and collect winnings
2. Update Dedicated Fans
3. Player advancement
4. Hiring, firing and temporary retirement
5. Expensive mistakes
6. Prepare for the next fixture

sections.heading: Winnings
sections.body: Added to the treasury: winnings = (Fan Attendance ÷ 2 + touchdowns scored + 1 if you didn't stall) × 10 000 gp. Fan Attendance is both teams' Fan Factors added together.

sections.heading: Dedicated Fans
sections.body:
Win — roll a D6; if it's ≥ your current Dedicated Fans, +1 (max 7).
Loss — roll a D6; if it's < your current Dedicated Fans, −1 (min 1).
Draw — no change.

sections.heading: Advancement
sections.body: Players earn Star Player Points (SPP) for completions, interceptions, casualties, touchdowns and the MVP award, then spend them on new skills and characteristic improvements — each of which raises the player's value. The full earning, cost and value tables are on the [[page:spp|SPP & advancement]] page.

sections.heading: Expensive mistakes
sections.body: After a league fixture, a treasury of 100 000 gp or more risks a D6 roll on a table whose severity scales with the hoard: Crisis Averted (nothing), Minor Incident (lose D3 × 10 000 gp), Major Incident (lose half), or Catastrophe (keep only 2D6 × 10 000 gp). Spend your gold, don't hoard it.

sections.heading: Leagues & tournaments
sections.body: A league is a season of fixtures in which teams persist and grow through this post-game sequence. A tournament is usually a one-off format — often fixed Team Value and "resurrection" (no lasting injuries between games).
